opinion.h4: JAMES, STATE TREASURER OF TEXAS, ET AL. v. GILMORE ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE NORTHERN DISTRICT OF TEXAS. No. 850. Decided January 15, 1968.
opinion.p: Crawford C. Martin, Attorney General of Texas, George M. Cowden, First Assistant Attorney General, and J. C. Davis, W. O. Shultz II and James C. McCoy, Assistant Attorneys General, and A. J. Carubbi, Jr., for appellants.
The motion to affirm is granted and the judgment is affirmed.
opinion.h4: SCHOOL COMM., CITY OF BOSTON v. BOARD OF EDUCATION, 389 U.S. 572 (1968)
opinion.p: 389 U.S. 572 SCHOOL COMMITTEE OF CITY OF BOSTON v. BOARD OF EDUCATION ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME JUDICIAL COURT OF MASSACHUSETTS. No. 759. Decided January 15, 1968.
Elliot L. Richardson, Attorney General of Massachusetts, and Howard M. Miller, Assistant Attorney General, for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
